content:
The provider was only one dependency, but when it slowed down, my whole API started behaving like the dependency was in control.

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by ashinJPG on UnsplashIn my previous article, I wrote about how Kubernetes exposed performance problems that my local machine never showed. This article continues from the same production lesson: the real world does not only test our code, it tests every dependency around it. If you want to read that first, you can find it here:
This time, the problem came from an external provider.
The provider was usually fast. Our Go API called it during a checkout flow, received a response, saved the result, and moved on. For weeks, the integration looked boring in the best possible way.
Then the provider slowed down.
At first, only a few requests timed out. Then retry logic started adding more pressure. More goroutines waited for HTTP calls. More clients retried from their side. The…
